title: centaurea boissieri subsp. mariolensis
intro: Centaurea boissieri subsp. mariolensis, also known as the Iberian knapweed or mariolense knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea boissieri plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea boissieri subsp. mariolensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the flowers or leaves in water or oil, and it has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
